reasoning: The spread sharply spiked to 1.09 on February 28 (up from 1.05 the prior day and 0.98 just over a week ago). Rapid, outsized daily widenings in high-grade corporate spreads historically tend to experience short-term mean reversion as markets digest the localized shock. Consequently, the probability of the spread being even higher than 1.09 in the short term (March 9) is likely below 50%. Following the explicit prompt instruction to increase uncertainty for more distant dates (making probabilities for longer horizons closer to 0.5), the forecasts are progressively stepped toward 0.5 as the horizon extends, reflecting the fading effect of the immediate mean-reverting downward pressure.
